studies.officialTitle: A Phase IIa, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of ASC30 Tablets and ASC30 Tablets A1 in Participants With Obesity or Overweight With Weight-Related Comorbidities

design.description: Seven-cohort parallel-group study evaluating two ASC30 tablet formulations and matching placebo.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: All gastrointestinal adverse events were mild or moderate, with no grade 3 or higher drug-related adverse events, no drug-related serious adverse events, and no hepatic safety signal reported.

sources.title: Ascletis' Oral Small Molecule GLP-1, ASC30, Demonstrated Placebo-Adjusted Weight Loss of 7.7% in Its 13-Week U.S. Phase II Study

studies.officialTitle: A Phase I/IIa, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of ASC30 Injection, for Subcutaneous Use in Participants With Obesity

design.description: Sequential single- and multiple-ascending-dose study of ASC30 subcutaneous formulations.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: In the latest 24-week phase 2 portion, there were no discontinuations due to adverse events; adverse events were mild to moderate, gastrointestinal adverse events were mild, and no hepatic safety signal was reported.

sources.title: Ascletis Announces Positive Topline Results from U.S. Phase II, 24-Week Study for Its Ultra-Long-Acting Subcutaneous Depot Formulations of ASC30 for Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Phase I Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of ASC30 Tablets in Participants With Obesity

design.description: Sequential single- and multiple-ascending-dose study with five single-dose levels and three 28-day titration regimens.

studies.population: Adults aged 18 to 65 years with obesity and body mass index 30 to 40 kg/m2, without diabetes or other clinically significant disease.
studies.followUpDuration: 7 days after the 28-day multiple-dose treatment period
studies.safetySummary: No serious adverse events or grade 3 or higher adverse events were reported in the multiple-ascending-dose cohorts; gastrointestinal events were mild to moderate and no hepatic safety signal was detected.

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group fixed target-dose trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related complication, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common and were mainly mild to moderate; treatment discontinuation increased with dose.

sources.title: Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight and Type 2 Diabetes

design.description: Parallel-group target-dose trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes; weight management was an explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were most common, were generally mild to moderate, and occurred primarily during dose escalation.

sources.title: Tirzepatide once weekly for the treatment of obesity in people with type 2 diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight Following Intensive Lifestyle Intervention

design.description: A 12-week intensive-lifestyle lead-in preceded the 72-week randomized period.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight who achieved at least 5% weight loss during an intensive lifestyle lead-in, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common and were primarily mild to moderate.

sources.title: Tirzepatide after intensive lifestyle intervention in adults with overweight or obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight for the Maintenance of Weight Loss

design.description: After a 36-week open-label tirzepatide lead-in, participants were randomized to continue maximum tolerated dose or switch to placebo for 52 weeks.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and a weight-related complication, without diabetes, who completed the tirzepatide lead-in.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were most common and were largely mild to moderate.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related complication, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were most common in both groups and were mainly mild to moderate during dose escalation.

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide for Maintenance of Body Weight Reduction in Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: After a 60-week maximum-tolerated-dose lead-in, participants were randomized to continue that dose, reduce to 5 mg, or switch to placebo for 52 weeks.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight who completed 60 weeks of tirzepatide treatment.
studies.safetySummary: The safety profile was consistent with prior tirzepatide studies; gastrointestinal events were the most common adverse events.

studies.overallDuration: At least 52 weeks; median follow-up 104 weeks
studies.safetySummary: Adverse events leading to treatment discontinuation occurred more frequently with tirzepatide and were mainly gastrointestinal.

sources.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adolescents with Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Semaglutide Helps People With Excess Body Weight Lose Weight

design.description: Parallel-group 5:1:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal events occurred in 70.8%, 61.2%, and 42.8% with semaglutide 7.2 mg, 2.4 mg, and placebo; dysaesthesia was more frequent at 7.2 mg.

sources.title: Once-weekly semaglutide 7.2 mg in adults with obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Semaglutide Helps People With Type 2 Diabetes and Excess Body Weight Lose Weight

design.description: Parallel-group 3:1:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity and type 2 diabetes; weight management was an explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: The safety profile was consistent with the GLP-1 receptor agonist class; gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common.

sources.title: Once-weekly semaglutide 7.2 mg in adults with obesity and type 2 diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Oral Semaglutide Helps People With Overweight or Obesity Lose Weight

design.description: Parallel-group trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were more frequent with oral semaglutide and were mostly mild to moderate.

sources.title: Oral Semaglutide at a Dose of 25 mg in Adults with Overweight or Obesity

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly as Adjunct to Intensive Behavioural Therapy in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group trial with intensive behavioural therapy and an initial low-calorie diet.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity Who Have Reached Target Dose During run-in Period

design.description: After a 20-week semaglutide run-in, participants continued semaglutide 2.4 mg or switched to placebo for 48 weeks.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes, who reached semaglutide 2.4 mg during run-in.

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Semaglutide Once-weekly in East Asian Subjects With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Four-arm parallel-group trial of semaglutide 2.4 mg, semaglutide 1.7 mg, and their dose-matched placebos.

studies.population: East Asian adults with obesity or overweight.

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly on Weight Management in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity.

design.description: Parallel-group trial with diet and physical-activity counselling.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight, with or without type 2 diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: Effect and Safety of Subcutaneous Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once Weekly Compared to Liraglutide 3.0 mg Once Daily on Weight Management in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Four-arm parallel-group trial; semaglutide-versus-liraglutide assignment was open-label, with each active treatment double-blinded against its dosing-frequency-matched placebo.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: Effect of Subcutaneous Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly Compared to Placebo in Subjects With Obesity and Knee Osteoarthritis

design.description: Parallel-group trial with reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity.

studies.population: Adults with obesity and knee osteoarthritis.

sources.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Persons with Obesity and Knee Osteoarthritis

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Subcutaneous Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly in Subjects With Obesity and Prediabetes

design.description: Parallel-group trial with reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity.

studies.population: Adults with obesity and prediabetes.

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly in Adults With Overweight and Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group trial.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight.

studies.officialTitle: Effect of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once Weekly on Function and Symptoms in Subjects With Obesity-related Heart Failure With Preserved Ejection Fraction

design.description: Parallel-group trial with study treatment added to standard care.

studies.population: Adults with obesity-related heart failure with preserved ejection fraction, without diabetes, receiving standard care.

studies.officialTitle: Effect of Semaglutide 2.4 mg Once-weekly on Function and Symptoms in Subjects With Obesity-related Heart Failure With Preserved Ejection Fraction, and Type 2 Diabetes

design.description: Parallel-group trial.

studies.population: Adults with obesity-related heart failure with preserved ejection fraction and type 2 diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: Weight Maintenance in Adolescents With Obesity; Long-Term Treatment With Semaglutide s.c.2.4 mg Once-weekly

design.description: Single-group long-term treatment study.

studies.population: Adolescents aged 12 to 15 years with obesity.

sources.title: STEP TEENS Weight Maintenance study record

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Oral Semaglutide 50 mg Once Daily in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity (OASIS 1)

design.description: Parallel-group trial.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Oral Semaglutide 50 mg Once Daily in East Asian Participants With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group trial.

studies.population: East Asian adults with obesity or overweight.

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of Oral Semaglutide 50 mg Once Daily in Chinese Adults With Overweight or Obesity (OASIS 3)

design.description: Parallel-group trial.

studies.population: Chinese adults with obesity or overweight, with or without type 2 diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: SELECT - Semaglutide Effects on Cardiovascular Outcomes in People With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group cardiovascular outcomes trial with treatment added to standard care.

studies.population: Adults aged 45 years or older with overweight or obesity and established cardiovascular disease, without diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: A Study to Evaluate UBT251 in Chinese Adults With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Four-arm dose-ranging trial.

studies.population: Chinese adults with overweight or obesity.
studies.safetySummary: The sponsor reported a safety profile consistent with the mechanism; detailed peer-reviewed safety results were not yet available.

studies.officialTitle: A Study Investigating Safety, Tolerability and Efficacy of UBT251 in Participants Living With Overweight or Obesity
studies.population: Participants living with overweight or obesity.

studies.officialTitle: A Randomised, Multiple Ascending Dose Trial Assessing Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics of ZP7570 Administered in Subjects With Overweight or Obesity
studies.population: Subjects with overweight or obesity.

studies.officialTitle: A Randomized, Double-blind, Phase 2, Dose-finding Trial of Once Weekly Petrelintide Compared With Placebo in Participants With Obesity or Overweight With Weight Related Comorbidities

design.description: Parallel-group, dose-finding trial of five once-weekly petrelintide dose groups versus placebo.

studies.population: Participants with obesity or overweight and weight-related comorbidities.

studies.officialTitle: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Phase 2 Trial of Once-Weekly Petrelintide Compared With Placebo in Participants With Overweight or Obesity and Type 2 Diabetes
studies.population: Participants with overweight or obesity and type 2 diabetes.

studies.officialTitle: A Phase II, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Dose-Finding Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Petrelintide Co-Administered With RO7795068 in Adults With Obesity or Overweight
studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight.
